rationale: Both candidates correctly diagnose the issue as a run-level mismatch (default core_model → dummy weights) rather than a real bug, matching the thread's 'invalid' resolution and citing the same core evidence (run_args.py default, _add_dummy_load_format, PR #4354). Y is more thorough: it cites a broader set of specific files/lines (runtime.py, assertions.py in addition to stage_config.py/run_args.py), explicitly clarifies that the full_model pytest marker is independent of --run-level (explaining why marking alone didn't help), and directly addresses the reporter's actual A100 environment versus the H100/MI325 hardware_test declaration plus practical repro requirements (video path, GPU memory) — points X omits entirely. X is correct and adequately grounded but more generic/templated, without engaging the reporter's specific hardware context.
